occupation: Licensed Practical and Licensed Vocational Nurses
notes: Optional intermediate step. 1-year diploma. Many go CNA→RN directly.
Core career destination. ADN (2yr) or BSN (4yr) from entry.
Specialization of RN. Hospital-based. No extra degree. Inherits RN outbound transitions.
Specialization of RN. ICU/CCU focus. Adds explicit exception: → CRNA.
Teaching track. Master's or Doctoral. Exits clinical practice.
Entry point. Police academy ~6 months. Must be hired by a department first.
Specialization of patrol. Forensic/clerical focus. Lower resilience score.
Investigative track. Promoted from patrol. No additional degree typically required.

occupation: First-Line Supervisors of Police and Detectives
notes: Management track. Promoted from patrol. Parallel to detective track.
Entry point. Transit agency academy. Direct public hire — not a promotion from municipal patrol.
Lateral exit from law enforcement. Private sector. Often ex-detective or ex-patrol.

occupation: Probation Officers and Correctional Treatment Specialists
notes: Adjacent lateral entry. Requires bachelor's. Different agency but overlapping career community.
